specialism.name: api-page
specialism.task: Write or update a hand-curated `@docs`-block API reference page for one module of EnsembleKalmanProcesses.jl. These pages live in `docs/src/API/` and are the authoritative source of the documented public API as it appears in the built docs site. Each page selects which exported symbols are documented, in what order, and under which H2 section headings. Unlike `@autodocs` (which sweeps everything automatically), these are hand-curated: new exported symbols must be added explicitly, and removed or renamed symbols must be pruned. The output is a page that builds without `@docs` warnings and covers every exported symbol of the target module exactly once.

workflow.step: Detect the existing template. Read 1–2 well-formed pages to calibrate: — `docs/src/API/EnsembleKalmanProcess.md` for the H2-grouped style (multiple sections with headings like "## Getter functions", "## Error metrics"). — `docs/src/API/Sampler.md` for the flat style (a single `@docs` block with no H2 groups). Note the `@meta CurrentModule = ...` opening block — it sets the module context for all `@docs` lookups on the page.

Identify the exported symbols of the target module: grep -E '^export' src/<Module>.jl Also check the main module file `src/EnsembleKalmanProcesses.jl` for any re-exports that are associated with the target module.

Read the current API page (if it exists): cat docs/src/API/<Module>.md For each exported symbol, check whether it already has a `@docs` entry on the page. Produce two lists: — Missing: exported symbols with no `@docs` entry. — Orphan: `@docs` entries whose symbol is no longer exported or whose docstring no longer exists in `src/`. For method-dispatched symbols (multiple `@docs` entries with different signatures, e.g. `get_obs(ekp::EnsembleKalmanProcess)` vs `get_obs(ekp::EnsembleKalmanProcess, iteration)`), check that all relevant dispatch variants are listed on separate lines.

Edit the page: — Add missing symbols. Decide whether they belong in an existing H2 group or need a new one. Heuristics for grouping (match whatever grouping already exists): * Constructor / primary struct → "## Primary objects and functions" * Accessor functions (`get_*`) → "## Getter functions" * Error / convergence metrics → "## Error metrics" * Algorithm-specific update functions → flat or a section matching the algorithm name. — Remove orphan entries. — Add or update method-specific signatures for any exported function where dispatch on argument types matters for the public API (list each signature on its own line inside the `@docs` block). — Keep the `@meta CurrentModule` block at the very top of the file.

Build the docs locally to confirm no new warnings: julia --project=docs docs/make.jl Common warnings to fix: — "missing docstring for Symbol" — the symbol is in `@docs` but has no docstring in `src/`. Add the docstring or remove the `@docs` entry. — "duplicate docstring" — the symbol appears more than once on the page or across pages. Remove the duplicate. Do not report the task complete if the build emits `@docs`-related errors.

rubric.criterion: Every symbol returned by `grep -E '^export' src/<Module>.jl` has a corresponding `@docs` entry on the page after the task is applied. No exported symbol is silently undocumented in the API reference.
No `@docs` entry references a symbol that is no longer exported or that lacks a docstring in the current source tree.

New symbols are placed under the appropriate existing H2 group (or a new group that follows the naming convention of existing groups). The H2 → `@docs` structure is consistent with the rest of the page.
The docs build produces no more `@docs`-related warnings after the edit than before.

For any function with multiple public dispatch variants (different argument types that a user would need to distinguish), each variant is listed on its own line inside the `@docs` block, matching the convention already established on the page.

formatting.rule: `@meta CurrentModule = EnsembleKalmanProcesses` block at the very top of the file, immediately after the H1 heading.
H1 = display name of the module or concept (sentence-case, may omit .jl suffix). H2 = section group names (sentence-case). No H3 inside API pages.
One symbol per line inside each `@docs` block. Never comma-separate on one line.

Method-specific signatures: list each dispatch variant on its own line, written as the full function call with typed arguments — e.g., `get_obs(ekp::EnsembleKalmanProcess)` and `get_obs(ekp::EnsembleKalmanProcess, iteration)` on separate lines. Use the exact signature form that Documenter.jl matches.
No prose, no admonitions, no code fences on API pages — just the `@meta` block, H1, optional H2 groups, and `@docs` blocks.
formatting.example:
## Template for a flat API page (few symbols, no grouping needed):
# My Module
```@meta CurrentModule = EnsembleKalmanProcesses ```
```@docs MyType my_function my_other_function(::MyType) my_other_function(::MyType, ::Int) ```
## Template for an H2-grouped API page (many symbols, logical groupings):
# My Module
```@meta CurrentModule = EnsembleKalmanProcesses ```
## Primary objects
```@docs MyType construct_my_type update_my_type! ```
## Getter functions
```@docs get_field_a get_field_b(::MyType) get_field_b(::MyType, ::Int) ```
## [Error metrics](@id my_errors_api)
```@docs compute_my_error get_my_error_metrics ```
